articles.title: Polymarket bettors put 66% chance of US forces entering Iran by end of April
articles.summary: Polymarket traders are betting on a 66% probability that American military forces will be deployed to Iran before April 30th, signaling heightened expectations of Middle East escalation. The market reflects growing geopolitical tensions that could trigger major oil price swings, defense stock rallies, and broader market volatility that smart money is already positioning for.

articles.title: Inside the world of political betting: ‘My job for the rest of my life’
articles.summary: A bettor has turned political wagering into their full-time career, suggesting the market has matured enough to support professional gamblers who treat elections like sports. This signals political betting is evolving from a hobby into a legitimate income source, which means more sophisticated analysis and potentially tighter spreads as pros crowd out casual money.

articles.title: Analysis | Taking a gamble on prediction markets
articles.summary: Someone's analyzing whether prediction markets are worth the risk, probably weighing their accuracy against traditional polls or expert forecasts. The timing matters because mainstream media coverage like this drives new money into prediction markets, which means more liquidity and potentially better odds for sharp bettors.

articles.title: Gemini loses $589M in 2025, prediction market future not so bright
articles.summary: Gemini reported a staggering $589M loss in 2025, adding another bruise to the crypto exchange's already battered reputation. The bleeding suggests institutional money remains skittish about crypto-adjacent platforms, which could make prediction markets' push for mainstream adoption even steeper uphill.

articles.title: Exclusive | Lawmakers to Introduce Bipartisan Bill Banning Sports Bets on Prediction Markets
articles.summary: Bipartisan lawmakers are preparing legislation that would prohibit prediction markets from offering betting on sports events. The bill threatens to cut off a major revenue stream for platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket, potentially forcing them to rely entirely on political and economic predictions to stay profitable.

articles.title: Opinion | We Shouldn’t Want a World Run by Prediction Markets
articles.summary: Someone's arguing that prediction markets shouldn't become the dominant force in decision-making across society. This matters because as markets gain mainstream adoption, pushback from traditional institutions and policymakers could shape how much influence they're allowed to have in everything from corporate strategy to government policy.

articles.title: A very 2026 weekend at the D.C. bar where bettors could ‘monitor the situation’
articles.summary: A DC bar hosted bettors tracking real-time election results and market movements during what appears to be a pivotal 2026 midterm weekend. The venue represents the growing mainstream acceptance of prediction markets as both entertainment and serious political analysis tools.

articles.title: If one trader can force the outcome of a prediction market, it shouldn’t be tradable
articles.summary: A single trader with enough capital can manipulate prediction markets by forcing outcomes through their actions rather than predicting them, essentially breaking the core mechanism that makes these markets valuable. This exposes a fundamental flaw in how we design prediction markets, since allowing outcome manipulation turns what should be information aggregation into expensive gambling on who has the deepest pockets.

articles.title: Best Prediction Markets for March Madness: Claim These Offers Now
articles.summary: March Madness brings out prediction market platforms competing for new users with sign-up bonuses and promotional offers worth hundreds of dollars. Smart bettors can stack these welcome bonuses across multiple platforms to maximize their bankroll before the tournament tips off.

articles.title: Prediction markets' latest 'Wild West' accusations: Users are getting suckered by insider trading
articles.summary: Critics are claiming prediction markets have become a free-for-all where insiders with privileged information are cleaning out regular users who don't know they're playing a rigged game. If these accusations stick, they could trigger the kind of regulatory crackdown that would either force platforms to implement costly safeguards or scare away the retail money that keeps these markets liquid.

articles.title: Polymarket’s 5-cent signal was the only thing that got the Netanyahu rumors right
articles.summary: A 5-cent bet on Polymarket correctly predicted Netanyahu-related news that traditional media and other forecasting methods missed entirely. This shows how even tiny-money prediction markets can surface real signal from noise, proving that betting incentives work even when the stakes are minimal.

articles.title: You can bet on climate disasters. Business is booming.
articles.summary: Climate catastrophe bonds and weather derivatives are seeing massive growth as investors pile money into betting on hurricanes, floods, and other natural disasters. The surge signals that prediction markets are expanding beyond politics and sports into literally life-and-death events where information asymmetries and climate data create serious profit opportunities for sharp bettors.
